name: Estadio Azteca
tagline: The only stadium on earth to host three World Cups, and the first ever to host three opening matches.

why.title: Cathedral of Football
why.text: The pitch where both Pelé (1970) and Maradona (1986) lifted the World Cup. No other stadium can say that.

why.title: Three-Cup Record
why.text: 1970, 1986, and now 2026. The Azteca is the only venue ever to host matches at three different World Cups.

why.title: The Altitude Weapon
why.text: At 7,200 feet the air is thin, the ball flies farther, and unacclimatized opponents fade. El Tri is built for it.

why.title: A Wall of Noise
why.text: Steep two-tier stands sunk into volcanic rock keep even upper-deck fans close and trap a deafening roar.

gettingThere: Do not drive or rideshare to the doors — the roads collapse on matchdays. Ride transit and walk the last stretch in.
transit: Tren Ligero (Xochimilco Light Rail) to the 'Estadio Azteca' stop is the closest, smartest arrival — it feeds straight into the concourses.
Transfer from Metro Line 2 (blue) at Tasqueña onto the light rail; General Anaya is an alternate walk-in point.
Seven signed pedestrian corridors open at 7:00 AM, each a 10–30 minute walk to the stands.
Load a Tarjeta MI (Integrated Mobility Card, ~MXN $15 + balance) at any Metro booth to ride everything.
matchday: The official FIFA Fan Festival takes over the Zócalo (June 11–July 19) — free entry, giant screens, and live artists, expected to be one of the tournament's busiest. Around the ground, the football-mad Santa Úrsula barrio and its street market pile right up to the gates.
food: Santa Úrsula tianguis (San Benjamín St near Gate 3) — taco vendors, garnachas, and birria packed around the stadium.
Steamed tacos sold from baskets on bicycles outside the gates — cheaper and better than concourse food.
Carnitas Gudiño (Calle San Alejandro #36) — a local institution for slow-cooked pork.
Michoacaníssimo — beloved birria spot serving since 1988.

food.name: Birria El Michoacaníssimo
food.where: San Valentín 866, Pedregal de Santa Úrsula
food.note: Crowded weekend birria institution; consomé and quesabirria
why: Quesabirria and consomé worth the line
Local since 1988, steps from gates

food.name: Carnitas Gudiño
food.where: San Alejandro 36, Pedregal de Santa Úrsula
food.note: Don Beto's costilla tacos, 34 years strong
why: Slow-cooked costilla tacos are the move
34-year neighborhood institution

food.name: Las Muñecas
food.where: Calle Toltecas, Pedregal de Santo Domingo, Coyoacán
food.note: Suadero tacos 6pm-6am, red or green salsa
why: Open 6pm to 6am — built for evening kickoffs
Cheap suadero tacos, pick your salsa

food.name: Santa Úrsula tianguis (basket-bike tacos)
food.note: Steamed canasta tacos from bikes; beat concourse food
why: Cheaper and better than concourse food
Right at Gate 3, no walk

pre.name: FIFA Fan Festival CDMX
pre.where: Zócalo (Plaza de la Constitución), Historic Center; Metro Zócalo (Line 2)
why: Free entry, official FIFA fan zone
30-meter giant screen, live music

pre.name: El Hijo del Cuervo
pre.note: Iconic Coyoacán bar on the plaza, barrel beer, live music
why: Bohemian Coyoacán institution on Jardín Centenario
Cerveza de barril and live music before kickoff

pre.name: Sport and Chips Coyoacán
pre.where: Av. Miguel Ángel de Quevedo, San Andrés 1144, Coyoacán
pre.note: Big-screen sports bar, beers flowing, rowdy
why: Big screens and a rowdy crowd
Beers flowing, classic sports-bar energy

pre.name: La Coyoacana
pre.note: Friendly cantina, roving musicians, weekend buzz
why: Roving musicians and weekend buzz
Friendly cantina close to stadium

pre.name: Salón Ríos
pre.where: Río Nilo 71, Colonia Cuauhtémoc, fan-gathering district
pre.note: Screens everywhere, El Tri faithful, arrive early
why: Screens everywhere, packed El Tri crowd
Colonia Cuauhtémoc cantina classic

post.name: Salón Covadonga
post.note: 1958 Asturian cantina, huge room, late nights
why: Cavernous 1958 Asturian cantina
Domino slap and late-night energy

post.name: Cantina El Centenario
post.note: 1948 favorite, free botanas, weekend live music
why: 1948 classic with free botanas
Weekend live music keeps it buzzing

post.name: La Bipo (ex La Bipolar)
post.note: DJs, live bands, karaoke, no cover
why: DJs, live bands, karaoke, no cover
Close-in Coyoacán party spot

post.name: Cervecería de Barrio
post.note: Beers and mariscos, packed when Mexico plays
why: Cold beers and big mariscos plates
Packed whenever Mexico plays

merch.name: AmeShop (Tienda Oficial Club América)
merch.where: Inside Estadio Azteca / Banorte, Santa Úrsula Coapa

merch.name: Nike Store Estadio Azteca
merch.where: Calzada de Tlalpan 3465, Estadio Azteca, Santa Úrsula Coapa

tips: Take the Tren Ligero, not a car — the light rail stop is the single smartest way in and out.
Arrive absurdly early for the Opener: the city says be in the stadium zone by 9:00 AM for the ~1 PM kickoff.
Land 1–2 days early and hydrate hard — 7,350 feet hits harder than the mild weather suggests.
Pack a packable rain shell; predictable storms roll in after 1 PM.
Eat outside the gates — the Santa Úrsula tacos, birria, and carnitas beat anything inside on price and flavor.
Bring a clear bag only, small cash, and your FIFA FAN ID ready; leave the backpack and DSLR at the hotel.
weather: Mild not hot (highs ~24–26°C) but it's rainy season — pack a light rain shell for the near-daily afternoon storms.
lore: 1970 World Cup Final: Brazil 4–1 Italy, the coronation of Pelé and arguably the greatest team ever assembled.
1986 vs England: Maradona scored the 'Hand of God' and the 'Goal of the Century' within four minutes — then lifted the trophy here.
The only stadium on earth where both Pelé and Maradona won a World Cup.
A concert legend too — Michael Jackson played five shows in 1993, alongside U2, McCartney, Bad Bunny, and Karol G.

transport.summary: CDMX's official matchday plan is transit-and-walk only: the 'Última Milla' perimeter around the stadium is closed to general traffic, so the smart move is the Tren Ligero light rail to the 'Estadio Azteca' stop, then a short signed walk in.

rail.name: Tren Ligero (Xochimilco Light Rail)
rail.detail: The closest, fastest, safest arrival — the stop feeds straight into the concourses. On matchdays a direct Tasqueña→Estadio Azteca service runs with no intermediate stops, open to ticketholders only.
rail.deal: ~MXN $3 (≈US$0.18) per ride on a Tarjeta MI

rail.name: Metro Line 2 (blue)
rail.detail: Ride Line 2 southbound to its Tasqueña terminus, then transfer to the Tren Ligero. General Anaya (Line 2) is an alternate walk-in point.

points.t: General Anaya is a walk-in point

rail.deal: ~MXN $5 (≈US$0.30) per ride — full Metro + light-rail trip is about MXN $8

bus.name: Metrobús Line 1 + Perisur shuttle
bus.detail: Ride Metrobús Line 1 south to Perisur, then a special Perisur–Estadio Azteca–Cañaverales matchday route with free transfer.
bus.deal: Tarjeta MI fare; complimentary transfer on the stadium leg

bus.name: Trolebús (electric)
bus.detail: Electric trolleybus connections feed the southern transit hubs near the stadium.

points.t: southern transit hubs near the stadium

bus.deal: ~MXN $7–22.50 by route, paid with Tarjeta MI

shuttle.name: Ride (official matchday shuttle)
shuttle.detail: Direct buses from 7 city points — Bellas Artes, CETRAM Chapultepec, Ángel de la Independencia, Palacio de los Deportes, Parque México, Estadio Olímpico Universitario, San Jerónimo — for MXN $350/person, departing every 15 min from 7:00 AM until ~2 hrs after the final whistle. Tarjeta MI.

transport.fromAirport: AICM/MEX: Metrobús Line 4 → San Lázaro → Metro Line 1 → Pino Suárez → Line 2 → Tasqueña → Tren Ligero. AIFA/NLU (far north): Suburban Train → Buenavista → Metro Line B → Garibaldi → Line 8 → Bellas Artes → Line 2 → Tasqueña → Tren Ligero — budget extra time, AIFA is distant.
transport.rideshare: Strongly discouraged to the doors — the Última Milla perimeter is closed to general traffic and Calzada de Tlalpan gridlocks on matchdays. Authorized taxi zones sit at 5 designated points outside the perimeter; after the match, walk several blocks out or ride the light rail north before summoning a car.
transport.bike: About 2,000 shared bikes and 2,000 scooters are deployed ~3 hours pre-match along the Calzada de Tlalpan cycle path for the final approach.
tips: Buy a Tarjeta MI (Integrated Mobility Card, ~MXN $15 card fee + balance) at any Metro/Tren Ligero booth or vending machine — it pays for everything; reload via the CDMX app.
The matchday Tren Ligero direct service and the perimeter only admit fans with a match ticket, so have it ready.
Allow 45–65 min from the central tourist neighborhoods, and arrive in the stadium zone by ~9:00 AM for the ~1 PM Opening Match.

parking.summary: There is effectively no fan parking at the stadium. Public parking has been eliminated inside the 'Última Milla' perimeter, which is closed to general vehicles on matchdays — the official guidance is to not drive in at all. If you must drive, use a remote Park & Ride lot and shuttle in.

lots.name: Park & Ride remote lots
lots.detail: Five remote sites — Campo Marte, Plaza Carso, Centro Comercial Santa Fe, Six Flags México, and Parque Ecológico de Xochimilco — with shuttles every 15 min from 7:00 AM. Pay only with Tarjeta MI.

parking.prepaid: Park & Ride is reserved/paid via the Tarjeta MI (cash or the CDMX app to load); no general on-site stadium parking is sold.
parking.accessible: Accessible arrivals are best via the Tren Ligero 'Estadio Azteca' station, which connects directly into the concourses and ramps; for wheelchair/mobility needs confirm with FIFA via the FIFA app. (Stadium-specific accessible-parking figures not published — confirm with FIFA.)
parking.tailgating: No tailgating — the immediate stadium zone is a pedestrian-only perimeter, not a parking-lot scene. The Santa Úrsula tianguis (street market) and taco vendors around the gates are the matchday gathering ground instead.
tips: Don't drive to the doors — the roads collapse and the perimeter is sealed; take the Tren Ligero instead.
If driving from afar, pick the Park & Ride lot nearest your route (e.g. Santa Fe from the west, Xochimilco from the south) and budget for the 15-min shuttle frequency.
Remote-lot and shuttle payment is Tarjeta MI only — load it before matchday.
